summary: Systems architect accepts M1 exit evidence. docs/GATE-M1.md records the M1 gate as PASS, with SIM-001 through SIM-006 CLOSED, SIM-005 integrated through PR #22 at 021c2545438af71455547abf9761cf671f39c280, closure PR #23 integrated at 3037107e04ac8619c44ae54abafa4766e9338104, local validation passing, PR Gate CI passing for #22 and #23, and explicit Roadmap gap classification for host hash evidence.

decisions: M1_GATE systems_architect result is PASS and should proceed to independent qa_reviewer acceptance.

Current M1 evidence satisfies the milestone acceptance matrix for command semantics, deterministic 3650-day run, Save Envelope v1, minimal read model, invariant/golden/protocol tests, corrupt save rejection, and key API tests.

Roadmap wording for Chrome/Node/Electron hash consistency is classified as PASS_WITH_INTERPRETATION plus FOLLOW_UP_REQUIRED: current evidence proves Node plus Worker-compatible hashes and CI browser/desktop smoke, but does not directly extract matching hashes from real Chrome and packaged Electron host paths.
Do not start M2 implementation from this writer handoff; lead should wait for qa_reviewer ACCEPT and normal integration gates.
risks: Add an explicit Chromium plus Electron host hash canary before M2 exit or earlier if platform-host determinism is claimed.

Lead should reconcile existing coordination-state drift: project/goal-mode-state.json still records last_main_commit 021c2545438af71455547abf9761cf671f39c280 while HEAD/origin/main are 3037107e04ac8619c44ae54abafa4766e9338104.
SIM-006 content-runtime to sim-core boot-boundary contract remains a follow-up for M2 world/content integration.
Known MODULE_TYPELESS_PACKAGE_JSON warning remains non-blocking in Worker fixture tests.
requested_action: qa_reviewer: independently review docs/GATE-M1.md, this handoff, the cited SIM-001 through SIM-006 handoffs, local validation evidence, PR #22/#23 CI evidence, and the Roadmap gap classifications; return ACCEPT, REQUEST_CHANGES, or BLOCK for M1-GATE.
